tagline: From GitHub issue to reviewed pull request — multi-agent orchestration on your session model
subtitle: Planners design, parallel workers build, and 22 domain specialists review every step. 18 skills, adaptive depth, persistent per-project memory.

layers.name: Project Analysis
layers.summary: Cache tech stack and conventions in .hyperflow/

layers.name: Task Triage
layers.summary: Classify the task (types, complexity, risk, ambiguity, flow profile, personas) before any worker fires

layers.name: Autonomy
layers.summary: Zero confirmations, minimal output, silent recovery

layers.name: Role Dispatch
layers.summary: Every agent runs on the current session model; roles differ by responsibility, not model.

layers.name: Task Templates
layers.summary: Pre-built decomposition: CRUD, API, UI, migration, refactor, debug

skills.name: scaffold
skills.purpose: Analyze project, create .hyperflow/ cache, install multi-tool shims

skills.name: plan
skills.purpose: Amplify the prompt, design the approach, and decompose into a batched task file (max thinking) — skips whichever phase the request doesn't need; stops at a build-location gate, never auto-implements

skills.name: issue
skills.purpose: Turn a GitHub issue into a triaged, planned, dispatched, and reviewed pull request — bug reports route through root-cause discipline, features through the plan chain; every outbound action gated

skills.name: dispatch
skills.purpose: Dispatch parallel workers + Worker → Reviewer review + final integration review; endpoint of the chain

skills.name: workflow
skills.purpose: Use Claude Code native workflows or the Codex/OpenCode portable adapter for system-wide work, large migrations, repo-wide audits, and high-confidence verification

skills.name: trace
skills.purpose: Systematic 5-Whys + hypothesis testing — never patches symptoms

skills.name: audit
skills.purpose: L1 quick → L5 exhaustive review on changes, files, or PRs

skills.name: pr
skills.purpose: Run the L1-L5 audit against an incoming pull request's real diff range, post findings as one batched GitHub review, chain fixes on NEEDS_FIX, and merge behind a gate — contributor code never executes without explicit consent

skills.name: deploy
skills.purpose: Lint, typecheck, build, tests, security sweep, commit, release, push (push always asks)

skills.name: cache
skills.purpose: Show, search, add, edit, prune, archive, clear, stats, migrate, off, compact

skills.name: handoff
skills.purpose: Manage a cross-environment two-session run — list/status/pickup/review/complete a committed handoff package (plan in one session, build in another, review back in the first)

skills.name: design
skills.purpose: Establish/extend the design system, research real-world prior art, dispatch the designer specialist, review for taste + accessibility — ends with a handoff gate into the chain

skills.name: status
skills.purpose: One-screen read-only view — version, profile freshness, memory count, live progress on every in-flight task

skills.name: background
skills.purpose: List, inspect, cancel, and prune background agents fired during prior chain runs

skills.name: sticky
skills.purpose: Set auto-routing: on (every task-shaped message routes), auto (intent-verb messages route — default), off

skills.name: bridge
skills.purpose: Embed the managed doctrine block into CLAUDE.md so autonomy + intent-routing + commit cadence carry to Desktop, claude.ai web, and IDE extensions

skills.name: flush
skills.purpose: Recover a deferred-commit queue from a prior or interrupted chain — fast-forward the staging branch, delete staging, clear the queue

skills.name: hyperflow
skills.purpose: Apply the orchestration doctrine in Codex, Antigravity, Grok, or another single-agent surface — auto-invoked for non-trivial engineering work

router.name: brain
router.focus: Selects the responsible specialist roster after triage; decides web-research + fan-out (cheap pass-through on fast/standard flows)

webResearch.note: Specialists research current best-practices/CVEs before judging on gated flows; cited, cached to memory, skipped offline

capabilities: Parallel worker dispatch (independent subtasks run simultaneously)
Big-task workflow routing for deep, scientific, system-wide, migration, audit, and verification-heavy tasks
Codex/OpenCode/Grok portable workflow adapter for big tasks
Reviewer review of every worker output (iron rule)
Domain-specialized reviewer & investigator agent registry (agents/) with web-research-first on gated flows
Brain adaptive specialist routing (decides the responsible roster once after triage; inherited downstream)
Recursive sub-agent fan-out (depth-capped at 1, budget-bounded)

Universal agent consultation: any agent (current or future) can ask a peer for help mid-task — workers via an orchestrator-brokered CONSULT signal, decision agents directly; depth-1, budget-bounded, never overrides a security halt
Two-session execution: plan in one session, build in another environment (Codex/Gemini/OpenCode/Grok), review back — via a git-committed handoff package
Multi-level review depth (L1-L5)
Systematic root-cause debugging
Project-scoped persistent memory with tag taxonomy
Hot/warm/cold memory tiering with user-invoked compaction (`/hyperflow:cache compact`) and automatic cold archiving
Lazy memory injection (only tag-matched entries per task)
Multi-tool auto-detection (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md)
Multi-provider support (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Antigravity, Cursor, Grok)
Auto-detect provider via env vars or folder presence
Every agent runs on the current session model; roles differ by responsibility
Quality gates (lint, typecheck, build, tests)
Security blocklists for sensitive files and destructive commands
Conventional commits + automated release versioning
Task tracking with incomplete-task recovery across sessions
Session-start update check (cached daily) with an in-session prompt to update
Smart pre-compaction guard allows automatic compact only after dispatch reaches its end gate, then snapshots recoverable state
Auto-archive of stale .hyperflow/{tasks,audits,specs} — daily-gated, promotes Learnings/Decisions/Anti-patterns to memory before moving the file to .hyperflow/archive/<type>/YYYY-MM/
